mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in >50% of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of high inflation signals supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during CPIAUCSL above 250 yields positive 20-day forward returns in >65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity that supports corporate revenue projections and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year sample or 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of positive payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 4.5 percent imposes financing constraints on highly leveraged firms, triggering sector rotation away from growth stocks and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of months where FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates inventory accumulation that precedes corporate earnings revisions and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth combined with negative RSAFS growth yields positive 20-day forward returns in >55% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals demand destruction that outweighs rate-cut expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during tightening periods generates positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of observed months.
